items.title: IOS-XE Basic Troubleshooting and Configuration Guide

steps.action: Check device reachability and basic connectivity.
steps.expected: Successful ping to the device and reachable neighbors.

steps.action: Verify interface status and configuration.
steps.expected: Interfaces are up/up with correct IP addressing.

steps.action: Check routing table and protocol adjacencies.
steps.expected: Expected routes present; neighbor adjacencies established.

steps.action: Review system logs and error messages.
steps.expected: No critical/emergency errors; relevant warnings identified.

steps.action: Apply a basic configuration (if needed).
steps.expected: Interface configured with IP, description, and enabled.

steps.action: Verify configuration persistence after reload.
steps.expected: Configuration persists and interface is up after reload.

steps.action: Verify Nexus service status and basic connectivity.
steps.expected: Nexus is running and reachable on expected ports.

steps.action: Check Nexus logs for errors or warnings.
steps.expected: No critical errors; logs show normal startup.

steps.action: Validate network and firewall settings.
steps.expected: Required ports (e.g., 8081, 8443) are open and not blocked.

steps.action: Confirm repository configuration and connectivity.
steps.expected: Repositories are in service and proxying correctly.

steps.action: Check storage and disk space for blob stores.
steps.expected: Sufficient disk space; blob stores are in service.

steps.action: Validate and repair database/index if needed.
steps.expected: Database/index status is healthy.

steps.action: Review and apply secure configuration settings.
steps.expected: TLS/SSL enabled with valid certs; admin credentials rotated.

steps.action: Restart Nexus and verify recovery.
steps.expected: Nexus returns to expected state with no errors.

steps.action: Verify physical connectivity and power
steps.expected: WLC is powered on, LEDs indicate normal operation, and Ethernet link LEDs are active.

steps.action: Access the WLC CLI via console or SSH
steps.expected: Successfully logged into the WLC command-line interface.

steps.action: Check system status and logs
steps.expected: No critical errors; system uptime and resources are healthy.

steps.action: Verify interface configurations
steps.expected: Management interface has correct IP, subnet, and gateway; AP-manager and virtual interfaces are properly configured.

steps.action: Check AP connectivity and join status
steps.expected: APs are in 'RUN' state and associated with the WLC.

steps.action: Validate wireless service profiles and SSIDs
steps.expected: Desired SSIDs are enabled and mapped to correct VLANs and security policies.

steps.action: Verify RADIUS and authentication servers
steps.expected: RADIUS servers are reachable and responding; authentication policies are applied.

steps.action: Check RF and channel configuration
steps.expected: Channels and power settings are optimal and non-overlapping; no channel congestion.

steps.action: Review client associations and performance
steps.expected: Clients are associated, authenticated, and achieving expected throughput.

steps.action: Save configuration and document changes
steps.expected: Running configuration is saved to startup configuration and change log is updated.

items.title: PAN-OS Basic Troubleshooting and Configuration Guide

steps.action: Check device connectivity and management access.
steps.expected: Management interface is reachable and admin can log in.

steps.action: Verify PAN-OS software version and availability of updates.
steps.expected: Device is running a supported PAN-OS version.

steps.action: Check interface status and IP configuration.
steps.expected: Interfaces are operational (up/up) with correct IP addressing.

steps.action: Review system logs for critical events.
steps.expected: No critical or repeated error entries.

steps.action: Validate security policies and rule hits.
steps.expected: Policies reflect intended allow/deny logic with expected hit counts.

steps.action: Check routing tables and next-hop reachability.
steps.expected: Preferred route is active and next-hop is reachable.

steps.action: Confirm NAT rules and translation behavior.
steps.expected: NAT rules match traffic intent and show expected translated addresses.

steps.action: Test end-to-end connectivity and application flow.
steps.expected: Traffic flows as expected; no drops due to security rules or NAT.

steps.action: Commit changes and verify configuration synchronization in HA pairs.
steps.expected: Commit succeeds and devices are synchronized in HA passive/active mode.
